objective: Bundle deterministic evidence classifier so LLM analyzer skips inline extension/content detection

changes.description: Classifies files under --evidence-dir by extension + 200-byte content peek. Outputs TSV (file_path, category, bytes, non_empty) + per-category summary. Supports --write-tsv to persist to $EVIDENCE_DIR/_classified.tsv.
Added Pre-classify instruction to Step 1: Discover Evidence. Inline prose left intact as fallback/reference.

verification.mock_evidence_run: PASS — all 10 fixture files classified correctly, 2 empty files flagged (non_empty=0)
verification.real_evidence_run: PASS — 122 files classified under e2e-evidence/, 16.3 MB total, 0 empty
verification.write_tsv_flag: PASS — writes e2e-evidence/_classified.tsv only when flag passed

categorization_accuracy_notes: screenshots (.png) and verdicts (VERDICT.md / report.md) classify deterministically
JSON peek disambiguates dom_snapshot (contains "html" or "document") vs api_response
*-stderr.txt and paths containing cli/ route to cli_output; other .txt/.log default to log
21 .data / .html files in the real evidence tree fall into 'unknown' — expected, not test evidence types
follow_ups: Consider adding .html classification if DOM snapshots get saved as .html instead of .json
Consider extending unknown-bucket files via `file(1)` MIME detection in a future pass
